groups.title: Total Insurance Incident Report
groups.desc:
Questions 1-3
Complete the form below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: Example                                            Answer Name                                               Michael Alexander Address                                           24 Manly Street, $1362$ , Sydney Shipping agent                             $1363$ Place of origin                                China Date of arrival                                $1364$ Reference number                          601 ACK

questions.title: Address 24 Manly Street,  【   】 , Sydney
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写地址中悉尼之前的地名。在听力原文中，Michael清楚地说明了他的当前地址是"24 Manly Street, Milperra near Sydney"。Milperra是位于悉尼附近的一个suburb（郊区），正好符合题目要求。此外，Michael还特意拼写了Milperra这个不常见的地名（M-I-L-P-E-R-R-A），这进一步确认了答案的准确性。在填写答案时，我们需要注意不要误填Sydney，因为题干中已经给出了Sydney，我们需要填写的是Sydney之前的地名。同时，我们也要确保正确拼写Milperra这个不常见的地名。

questions.title: Shipping agent  【   】
questions.analysis:
：
        1. 题目直接询问了"Shipping agent"（运输代理）是谁。
        2. 在对话中，Judy明确问道："Yes, the company who packed everything up at the point of origin."（是的，在起始点打包所有东西的公司。）这个描述与"Shipping agent"的定义相符。
        3. Michael回答说："Oh, it was ... er ... First Class Movers."，直接给出了运输公司的名称。
        4. "First Class Movers"是对话中唯一被提到的与运输相关的公司名称，符合题目要求的运输代理。
        5. 这个答案恰好符合题目要求的"NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS"（不超过三个词）的限制。

        因此，"First Class Movers"是正确的答案，它准确回答了题目关于运输代理的询问。

questions.title: Date of arrival  【   】
questions.analysis:
题目要求填写到达日期（Date of arrival）。在对话中，Judy询问了Michael关于货物运输的情况，Michael提供了两个日期：离开日期（10月11日）和到达日期（11月28日）。由于题目特别询问到达日期，因此正确答案应该是11月28日。

        需要注意的是，答案可以有多种正确的表达方式，如"November 28"、"28 November"、"28th November"或"November 28th"。这些都是可以接受的日期表达形式。重要的是要确保月份和日期的顺序正确，并且正确使用序数（如"28th"）。

        在听力考试中，要特别注意与日期相关的信息，尤其是当听到"got to"、"arrived"等表示到达的词语时。这些通常是信号词，提示接下来可能会提到我们需要的到达日期。

        另外，也要注意区分出发日期和到达日期。在这个例子中，如果不仔细听，可能会错误地写下10月11日这个出发日期。因此，保持专注并仔细区分不同的日期信息非常重要。

groups.desc:
Questions 4-10
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER in each gap.

content: The $1367$ of the cabinet is damaged

questions.analysis: 题目要求填写电视机被损坏的部分。在对话中，Michael提到电视机的屏幕（screen）有一个巨大的裂缝，导致它无法使用。"screen"是电视机的一个主要部件，而且是被直接提到的受损部分。虽然"crack"（裂缝）描述了损坏的方式，但题目要求填写的是被损坏的部分，而不是损坏的方式。因此，"screen"是最适合的答案。此外，"screen"也符合题目要求的"ONE WORD"规则。记住，在填空题中，我们需要根据题目要求准确提取所需的信息，而不是简单地填写所有听到的相关词汇。

题目要求填写的是橱柜的位置。在听力原文中，说话者明确提到了这个橱柜来自浴室（"The cabinet from the bathroom"）。虽然后面还提到了橱柜的用途（放毛巾），但题目要求的是位置，不是用途。因此，"bathroom"（浴室）是正确答案。

        在做这类题目时，要注意以下几点：
        1. 仔细阅读题目要求，明确需要填写的信息类型（如位置、用途、状态等）。
        2. 听力时要集中注意力，特别是在听到关键词（如本题中的"cabinet"）时。
        3. 不要被无关信息干扰。比如本题中提到的"towels"虽然与浴室有关，但不是题目所问的内容。
        4. 记住，答案通常会在关键词（如"cabinet"）出现后很快给出，所以要特别注意关键词后面的信息。
题目要求填写柜子被损坏的部分。在原文中，说话者明确指出柜子的背面和侧面都没问题，但门上有一个大洞且无法修复。因此，"door"（门）是柜子被损坏的部分，符合题目要求。此外，"door"也是柜子的一个重要组成部分，与题目中提到的"cabinet"（柜子）直接相关。选择"door"作为答案不仅准确描述了损坏情况，也符合题目的语境要求。

题目要求填写的是替换或修理的成本。虽然原文中提到了两个价格（$125和$140），但$140才是正确答案，原因如下：

1. 原文中提到"When I bought it last year I paid $125 for it"，这是过去购买的价格，不是当前替换的价格。

2. 接着说"But the one I've seen here in Sydney is a bit more expensive, it's $140"。这里的"the one I've seen here"指的是用来替换损坏橱柜的新橱柜，而$140就是这个新橱柜的价格。

3. 题目要求填写的是"Cost to repair/replace"，即修理或替换的成本。在这种情况下，我们应该选择替换的成本，也就是$140。

所以，尽管听力中提到了两个价格，但根据上下文和题目要求，$140才是正确的答案。这个题目考察了考生对细节的把握能力，以及在多个数字中选择正确答案的能力。

：
        1. 题目要求填写餐桌的哪个部分被损坏了。
        2. 原文中明确提到"one leg has completely split down the middle"（一条腿从中间完全裂开了）。
        3. "leg"（腿）是桌子的一个重要部件，符合题目要求的填空内容。
        4. 虽然原文还提到了"middle"（中间），但题目要求的是被损坏的部分，而不是损坏的位置，所以"leg"是更准确的答案。
        5. 这个答案简洁明了，正好符合题目要求的"ONE WORD"（一个词）。

        因此，"leg"是最符合题目要求和原文描述的答案。

题目要求填写被破坏的具体物品。在原文中，说话者明确提到"some plates were broken - six actually"（有些盘子破了 - 实际上是六个）。虽然整段话提到了一整套餐具（china set），包括杯子、碟子等，但只有盘子（plates）被明确指出是破损的。此外，题目中的表格要求填写的是"Six ____ were broken"，结合上下文，"plates"是最合适的答案。这个答案不仅符合听力内容，也符合题目要求的语法结构。

原因如下：
    1. 对话中提到有6个盘子被打破了，每个价值约10美元。
    2. Judy（接线员）通过简单计算得出总价值约为60美元（6 x 10 = 60）。
    3. Michael（客户）确认了这个计算是正确的。
    4. 题目要求的是损坏物品的总价值，而不是单个价格。
    5. 60美元是对话中明确提到并得到确认的最终总价值。

    这个问题不仅测试了你的听力能力，还考察了你对对话整体的理解和简单计算能力。即使你没有听懂所有细节，只要注意到最后的数字确认，也能正确回答这个问题。

transcript.en: JUDY: Good morning. Total Insurance.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：早上好！这里是TOTAL保险公司。

transcript.en: Judy speaking, how may I help you?
transcript.cn: 我是茱迪，请问有什么可以帮您？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: I recently shipped my belongings from overseas back here to Australia and I took out insurance with your company.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：最近从海外寄了一些东西来澳大利亚，当时我购买了你们公司的保险。

transcript.en: Some items were damaged during the move so I need to make a claim.
transcript.cn: 我寄的东西有一些在运送过程中损坏了，所以我现在要申请理赔。

transcript.en: What do I have to do?
transcript.cn: 有哪些手续要办呢？

transcript.en: JUDY: Okay, well first I need to get a few details about this.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：好的。我先来核对一些具体信息。

transcript.en: Can you give me your name please?
transcript.cn: 请问您的姓名是？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Yes. It's Michael Alexander.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：好的。迈克尔·亚历山大。

transcript.en: JUDY: Okay. And your address please?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：好的。请问您的住址是？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: My old address or my current one?
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：是我的旧住址还是现在的？

transcript.en: JUDY: Your current one.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：现在的。

transcript.en: MICHAEL: It's 24 Manly Street, Milperra near Sydney.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：在悉尼边上，米尔佩拉，曼利街24号。

transcript.en: JUDY: What was the suburb, sorry?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：是什么地方来着？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Milperra. M-I-L-P-E-R-R-A.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：米尔佩拉，M-I-L-P-E-R-R-A。

transcript.en: JUDY: Right. Now, who was the shipping agent Mr Alexander?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：好的。接下来，亚历山大先生，请问您的货运代理商是谁？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: You mean the company we used?
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：你是说我当时选的货运公司吗？

transcript.en: JUDY: Yes, the company who packed everything up at the point of origin.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：是的，最开始给您打包的那家公司。

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Oh, it was ... er ... First Class Movers.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：哦，我选的是……呃……是First Class Movers。

transcript.en: JUDY: Okay ... where were the goods shipped from?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：好的……是从哪儿寄过来的？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: China, but the ship came via Singapore and was there for about a week.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：是从中国寄来的，不过货轮经过了新加坡并且在那儿停了大概一周。

transcript.en: JUDY: Don't worry, all of that information will be in the documentation. Now, the dates.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：没事儿，这些信息存档里应该都有。接下来是日期。

transcript.en: Do you know when the ship arrived?
transcript.cn: 您知道货轮什么时候到达的吗？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: It left on the 11th of October and got to Sydney on the 28th of November.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：货船10月11日离开，11月28日到的悉尼。

transcript.en: JUDY: Okay. I need one more thing.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：好的。我还需要最后一项信息。

transcript.en: There's a reference number.
transcript.cn: 我需要一个编号。

transcript.en: It should be in the top right-hand corner of the pink form they gave you.
transcript.cn: 应该在粉色的运单的右上角。

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Let me have a look.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：我来找找看。

transcript.en: I have so many papers.
transcript.cn: 我这儿单子太多了。

transcript.en: Yes, here it is. It's 601 ACK.
transcript.cn: 啊，有了。是610 ACK。

transcript.en: JUDY: Thanks.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：谢谢。

transcript.en: JUDY: I need to take down a few details of the actual damage over the phone before you put in a full report.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：现在我还需要记录一下物品破损细节，然后就可以提交完整的报告了。

transcript.en: Can you tell me how many items were damaged and what the damage was?
transcript.cn: 请您能说一下损坏物品和损坏详情吗？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Yes, well four things actually.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：好的。总共有四件物品。

transcript.en: I'll start with the big things.
transcript.cn: 我先从大件说起吧。

transcript.en: My TV first of all. It's a large one ... very expensive.
transcript.cn: 首先是我的电视机。是个大屏幕的……很贵的。

transcript.en: JUDY: Our insurance doesn't cover electrical problems.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：我们的保险项目不包括电子故障。

transcript.en: MICHAEL: It isn't an electrical problem.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：那不是电子故障。

transcript.en: The screen has a huge crack in it so it's unusable.
transcript.cn: 屏幕上裂了一个大口子，完全没法用了。

transcript.en: JUDY: I see. Any idea of the price to repair it?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：我知道了。您知道维修费用是多少吗？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: No. Well, I don't think it can be repaired.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：不知道。而且我不觉得这个能修好。

transcript.en: It will need a new one.
transcript.cn: 得换个新的。

transcript.en: JUDY: Okay. I'll make a note of that and we'll see what we can do.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：好的。我记录一下，然后视情况而定。

transcript.en: Now, what was the second item?
transcript.cn: 第二件物品是什么呢？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: The cabinet from the bathroom was damaged as well.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：浴室柜也坏了。

transcript.en: It's a lovely cabinet, we use it to keep our towels in.
transcript.cn: 那个柜子很精致，是放毛巾用的。

transcript.en: JUDY: And what is the extent of the damage?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：损坏程度如何呢？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Well, the back and the sides seem okay but the door has a huge hole in it.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：呃，后面和侧面看着还行，但是门上有一个大洞。

transcript.en: It can't be repaired. I'm really not very happy about it.
transcript.cn: 这没法修复的。我真的很郁闷。

transcript.en: JUDY: And how much do you think it will cost to replace it?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：那您觉得换一个新的要多少钱呢？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Well, when I bought it last year I paid $125 for it.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：这个，我是去年买的，当时花了125美元。

transcript.en: But the one I've seen here in Sydney is a bit more expensive, it's $140.
transcript.cn: 但是我在悉尼看到的同款更贵一些，是140美元。

transcript.en: JUDY: Right, and what was the third item?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：好的，那第三件呢？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: My dining room table. It's a lovely table from Indonesia.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：是我的餐桌。那是一张印尼产的很可爱的餐桌。

transcript.en: It must have been very hot inside the container because one leg has completely split down the middle.
transcript.cn: 我估计是集装箱里面温度太高了，桌子的一条腿从中间开裂了。

transcript.en: The top and the other three look okay thank goodness,
transcript.cn: 好在桌面和其他三条腿没事儿。?

transcript.en: JUDY: Any idea of the price to repair it?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：您知道修复需要花费多少吗？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Well, I had an estimate done on this actually because it is a very special table to us.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：我已经找人估算过了，因为这张桌子对我们来说太特别了。

transcript.en: They quoted us $200, which is really pricey so I hope the insurance will cover the total cost.
transcript.cn: 他们的报价很高，200美元，所以我希望保险能够涵盖所有的费用。

transcript.en: JUDY: I'm sure that will be fine.
transcript.cn: 茱迪：这应该没有问题。

transcript.en: What was the last item, Mr Alexander?
transcript.cn: 最后一项是什么，亚历山大先生？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Well, we have a lovely set of china plates and dishes, you know, with matching cups, saucers, the lot.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：我们有一套瓷的餐具，就是那种带配套的杯子啊碟子啊的那种全套的。

transcript.en: They were all in the one box which must have got dropped because some plates were broken - six actually.
transcript.cn: 它们被打包在一个箱子里，然后这个箱子肯定是摔过了，因为有6个碟子碎了。

transcript.en: JUDY: And can you tell me the replacement value of these?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：您能告诉我换新的费用吗？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Well, it's hard to say because they were part of a set but they can be up to $10 each as it's such a good set.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：这个不好说，因为它们是套装里的，但我觉得每个碟子的价格差不多在10美元左右，因为这是一套很高档的套装。

transcript.en: JUDY: Okay, so that would be around $60 altogether?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：好的，那么一共是大概60美元对吧？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Yes, that's right.
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：是的。

transcript.en: JUDY: And is that all of the items?
transcript.cn: 茱迪：就这些了吗？

transcript.en: MICHAEL: Yes. So what do I have to do now?
transcript.cn: 迈克尔：是的。那接下来我要做些什么呢？

groups.desc:
Question 11
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: According to the speaker, the main purposes of the park are
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B（research and education）。原文中明确指出了公园的两个主要目的：为公众提供教育机会（education）和为科学家提供研究场所（research）。这两个目的直接对应了选项B中的"research and education"。原文使用"remit"一词，意为"职责"或"任务"，在此处等同于"main purposes"，因此这句话直接回答了题目所问的问题。

groups.title: Agricultural Park
groups.desc:
Questions 12-14
Label the plan below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

questions.analysis: 答案是“Forest”或“the Forest”。因为在听力材料中明确提到“further east ... in the largest section of our Park is the Forest Area”，从方位上进一步东侧且最大的区域就是森林区。因此，正确答案为“Forest”。

第13题的正确答案是“Fish Farm”或“Fish Farms”，因为听力原文明确提到“these two small rectangular shapes here ... are the Fish Farms where we rear fish for sale”，并且这些矩形形状位于湖泊旁边。通过定位词“Lake”和信号词“circular area is our lake”，可以准确找到答案位置。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过关注地图和抓住关键词来推测正确答案。
题目第14题要求填入一个区域的名称。根据听力原文，明确提到了“south-eastern corner”（东南角）和“beyond the marsh”（沼泽以外），然后指出该区域是“Market Garden area”（菜园区）。由于题目第14题位置与地图上的东南角匹配，并且满足“beyond the marsh”这一描述，因此正确答案是“Market Garden”。

groups.desc:
Questions 15-20
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: When are the experimental areas closed to the public?
questions.analysis: 正确答案是C（每年短时间），因为听力材料中明确提到实验区域只是在进行特定控制实验时才"暂时"（temporarily）对公众关闭。这表明关闭时间是短暂的，而不是全年或几乎全年。关键词"temporarily out of bounds"直接表明了实验区域只是暂时关闭，与选项C"a short time every year"最为吻合。

questions.title: How can you move around the park?
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A选项"by tram, walking or bicycle"。原文中明确提到了三种在公园内移动的方式：电动有轨电车（electric tram）、步行（go on foot）和自行车（bicycles）。这些方式都符合环保原则，且原文强调了汽车是被禁止的。A选项准确地概括了这三种移动方式，因此是正确答案。

questions.title: The rare breed animals kept in the park include
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C选项"goats and hens"。在原文中，讲话者明确提到他们饲养的稀有品种动物包括山羊（goats）和母鸡（hens）。虽然原文还提到了绵羊和其他家禽，但选项C最准确地反映了题目所问的内容。值得注意的是，原文还提到他们正在考虑引入牛和马，但目前还没有这些大型动物的设施，因此这些动物不能被视为公园目前饲养的稀有品种动物。

questions.title: What is the main purpose of having the Rare Breeds Section?

options.title: B
options.content: to keep a variety of breeds

questions.analysis: 正确答案是B "to keep a variety of breeds"。原文中明确指出，Rare Breeds Section的主要目的是"to maintain the diversity of breeds"，这与选项B直接对应。文中强调这一目的是为了"broaden the gene pool for agricultural development"，即通过保持品种多样性来扩大农业发展的基因库。这个解释排除了选项A和C，同时也进一步支持了选项B的正确性。

questions.title: What can you see in the park at the present time?
questions.analysis: 这道题考察了考生对当前正在进行的活动的理解能力。正确答案是C，因为原文明确提到约翰·哈弗斯"目前正在"（currently）进行钓鱼示范。这与题干中的"at the present time"（目前）相对应，表明这是当前正在进行的活动。选项C中的"demonstration of fishing"（钓鱼示范）与原文中的"giving displays"（进行展示）在语义上是一致的。

questions.title: The shop contains books about
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A（animals）。原文提到商店里有"a wide selection of books on wildlife"（各种关于野生动物的书籍）。"Wildlife"（野生动物）直接对应了选项中的"animals"（动物）。虽然原文也提到了当地作者和农业历史，但这些并不直接对应B或C选项。因此，A选项最准确地反映了商店中书籍的主要内容。

transcript.en: Welcome to Green Vale Agricultural Park.
transcript.cn: 欢迎来到格林维尔农业公园。

transcript.en: As you know, we have only been open a week so you are amongst our first visitors.
transcript.cn: 大家都知道我们这里开业才一周，所以你们可以算是我们的首批游客了。

transcript.en: We have lots of fascinating indoor and outdoor exhibits on our huge complex, spreading hundreds of hectares.
transcript.cn: 这个公园占地数百公顷，有无数吸引人的户外和室内展览。

transcript.en: Our remit is to give educational opportunities to the wider public as well as to offer research sites for a wide variety of agriculturists and other scientists.
transcript.cn: 我们为广大民众提供受教育机会，同时也为农业和其他科学家提供研究场所。

transcript.en: Let's start by seeing what there is to do.
transcript.cn: 我们先来看看在这里大家都能做些什么。

transcript.en: As you can see here on our giant wall plan, we are now situated in the Reception block ... here.
transcript.cn: 我们来看看这幅巨大的墙壁平面图，我们现在位于接待中心，在这儿。

transcript.en: As you walk out of the main door into the park there is a path you can follow.
transcript.cn: 进了大门之后有一条路。

transcript.en: If you follow this route you will immediately come into the Rare Breeds section, where we keep a wide variety of animals which I shall be telling you a little more about later.
transcript.cn: 沿着这条路往里走你很快就会走到动物饲养区。我在那儿饲养了很多种动物，一会儿我会给大家细说。

transcript.en: Next to this ... moving east ... is the large grazing area for the rare breeds.
transcript.cn: 紧挨着饲养区的东边是一大片牧场。

transcript.en: Then further east ... in the largest section of our Park is the Forest Area.
transcript.cn: 继续往东走是为稀有品种提供的大片牧区。

transcript.en: South of the grazing area and in fact just next to the Reception block is our Experimental Crop Area.
transcript.cn: 在牧场的南边，挨着接待中心的位置是我们的农作物实验区。

transcript.en: In the middle of the Park ... this circular area is our lake ....
transcript.cn: 在公园的中部，这个圆形的区域是一个湖……

transcript.en: These two small rectangular shapes here ... are the Fish Farms where we rear fish for sale.
transcript.cn: 这两个小型的长方形的区域是养渔场，我在那里养鱼来售卖。

transcript.en: To the east of those is the marsh area which attracts a great many migrant birds.
transcript.cn: 渔场的东边是一片湿地，那里栖息着很多候鸟。

transcript.en: In the south-eastern corner, beyond the marsh, is our Market Garden area, growing vegetables and flowers.
transcript.cn: 穿过湿地，在公园的东南角是我们的商品菜园，里面种植了很多蔬菜和花卉。

transcript.en: All these areas can be visited by the general public for almost all the year ... although ... please take note of the large signs at the entrance to each area which tell...which tell you when certain areas are being used for particular controlled experiments and are therefore temporarily out of bounds to the public.
transcript.cn: 所有这些区域几乎全年对外开放，但是请大家在进入到每个区域的时候留意一下门口的巨大的标示牌。有些区域在某些特定的时间会被用来做受控实验，在那段时间暂时不对公众开放。

transcript.en: You can see for yourself what a huge area the park covers and a key question is always, how can we move around?
transcript.cn: 大家应该能看到公园面积广阔，那么突出的问题就是我们该什么哪种交通方式游览观光。

transcript.en: Well you have a choice of means ... all environmentally friendly ... cars are banned in the park.
transcript.cn: 你有几种选择，每一种都很环保……，公园内是禁止车辆通行的。

transcript.en: We have bicycles which you can hire behind the Reception block ... here ... the healthy ones of you can go on foot and finally there's our electric tram, powered from solar cells.
transcript.cn: 接待中心后面……在这儿……我们提供自行车出租；如果您体力充沛的话可以选择步行。最后我们还有太阳能驱动的电车。

transcript.en: You find more information about this at the front entrance.
transcript.cn: 在前台您可以获得更多信息。

transcript.en: A good place to start on your tour is the Rare Breeds section.
transcript.cn: 我建议大家先从动物养殖区开始参观。

transcript.en: We keep goats, sheep and hens and other kinds of poultry.
transcript.cn: 我们养了山羊、绵羊、母鸡和其他的家禽。

transcript.en: We are also thinking of bringing in cows and horses but we do not, as yet, have facilities for these bigger animals.
transcript.cn: 未来我们还将考虑养殖牛和马，但目前我们暂时还没有饲养大型动物的设施。

transcript.en: The animals are fed in public twice a day and a short lecture given on their feeding habits and nutritional needs.
transcript.cn: 这些动物每天当众喂养两次，在这个过程中会有一个小型的关于它们的进食习惯和营养需求的讲座。

transcript.en: These are very popular with the public but of course we mustn't lose sight of the main purpose of having this section, not as such to preserve rare animals but to maintain the diversity of breeds to broaden the gene pool for agricultural development.
transcript.cn: 这个活动很受欢迎。当然，我们不能忘了这个养殖区的建立不是为了保护这些动物，而是保持种群的多样性，丰富基因库，以促进农业发展。

transcript.en: Green Vale changes with the seasons with different events happening at different times of the year.
transcript.cn: 随着季节变化，园区会在一年中的不同时间举办不同的活动。

transcript.en: May will be perhaps our most spectacular month with the arrival of the Canada geese and when our fruit trees will be in full blossom, but there are interesting events on all year round ... for example John Havers, our expert fly fisherman is currently giving displays on the lake.
transcript.cn: 五月是我们这儿最热闹的时候。那会儿加拿大野雁正好回来了，公园里的果树也开花了。不过我们全年都有很多有趣的活动，比如最近我们的飞钓专家约翰·哈维斯就在湖区给大家展示钓鱼技巧。

transcript.en: Each of the sections has its own seasonal calendar ... please consult the summary board at the main entrance.
transcript.cn: 每一个区域都有独立的季节性活动安排……，大门口的公示牌上有具体信息。

transcript.en: And the final section, as we return to the Reception blocks, is the orchard.
transcript.cn: 我们在返回接待中心的时候回经过最后一块区域，果园。

transcript.en: Do take time to browse round our shop ... there is a wide selection of books on wildlife, some of them written by local authors, and the history of farming, including organic farming, something which the park will be diversifying into in the coming months.
transcript.cn: 建议您抽点时间逛一逛我们的商店，那里能买到很多关于野生动物的书籍，其中有一些是当地作者写的。还有很多书是关于农业的，包括有机农业。我们在今后的几个月也会引进这些不同的农业模式。

groups.title: Honey Bees in Australia
groups.desc:
Questions 21-24
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Where in Australia have Asian honey bees been found in the past?
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A. Queensland。在听力材料中，讲话者明确提到他们曾经在昆士兰（Queensland）发现过亚洲蜜蜂，并将其根除。这句话直接回答了题目中询问的"在澳大利亚的哪里曾经发现过亚洲蜜蜂"。虽然材料中也提到了新南威尔士州和其他州，但那是在讨论未来可能的情况，而不是过去已经发生的事实。

questions.title: A problem with Asian honey bees is that they
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B（carry parasites）。在原文中，演讲者明确指出亚洲蜜蜂被螨虫（mites）感染。螨虫是一种微小的寄生虫，符合"parasites"的定义。这是亚洲蜜蜂的一个直接问题，而不是它们可能造成的间接影响。题目询问的是亚洲蜜蜂本身的问题，而不是它们可能导致的后果。因此，选项B最准确地回答了问题。

questions.title: What point is made about Australian bees?

options.title: B
options.content: Their size stops them from pollinating some flowers.

options.title: C
options.content: They are sold to customers abroad.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C选项"They are sold to customers abroad"。原文中明确提到澳大利亚向多个国家出口本土蜂王，这与选项C中"sold to customers abroad"的含义完全一致。这里的"export"（出口）与"sold"（销售）在语境中是同义的，而"a large number of countries"（许多国家）对应了"customers abroad"（海外客户）。因此，C选项最准确地概括了原文中关于澳大利亚蜜蜂的这一重要信息。

questions.title: Grant Freeman says that if Asian honey bees got into Australia,

options.title: A
options.content: the country's economy would be affected.

options.title: B
options.content: they could be used in the study of allergies.

options.title: C
options.content: certain areas of agriculture would benefit.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A，因为Grant Freeman明确指出如果亚洲蜜蜂进入澳大利亚，将会对国家经济产生影响。他提到本地蜜蜂每年为农作物授粉创造的价值达12亿美元，如果亚洲蜜蜂入侵，可能会导致这部分经济价值的损失。这直接说明了亚洲蜜蜂的入侵会影响澳大利亚的经济。

groups.title: Looking for Asian honey bees
groups.desc:
Questions 25-30
Complete the summary below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect:
Birds called Rainbow Bee Eaters eat only   $1375$ , and cough up small bits of skeleton and other products in a pellet.
Researchers go to the locations the bee eaters like to use for   $1376$ . They collect the pellets and take them to a   $1377$ . for analysis. Here   $1378$ is used to soften them, and the researchers look for the   $1379$ of Asian bees in the pellets.
The benefit of this research is that the result is more   $1380$ than searching for live Asian bees.

questions.title: Looking for Asian honey bees    Birds called Rainbow Bee Eaters eat only   【   】 , and cough up small bits of skeleton and other products in a pellet.
questions.analysis: 原文中明确指出食蜂鸟吃的是昆虫（insects）。题目要求填写食蜂鸟吃的东西，而原文中直接说明了"as long as they're insects"，即只要是昆虫就可以。这个答案不仅直接对应了题目的要求，而且也是原文中唯一明确提到的食蜂鸟的食物。虽然整段讨论主要围绕蜜蜂展开，但食蜂鸟并不仅仅吃蜜蜂，而是吃各种昆虫。因此，"insects"是最准确和全面的答案。

questions.title: Researchers go to the locations the bee eaters like to use for   【   】 . They collect the pellets and take them to a   【   】 . for analysis. Here   【   】 is used to soften them, and the researchers look for the   【   】 of Asian bees in the pellets.
questions.analysis: 原文中直接提到了"favourite feeding spots"和"places where the birds usually feed"。这些短语与题干中的"locations the bee eaters like to use"在意义上完全对应。"feeding"这个词准确描述了蜜蜂食鸟在这些地点进行的活动。虽然"eating"在语义上也可以理解,但原文使用的是"feeding",因此"feeding"是更精确的答案。此外,原文中使用了同义词替换的技巧,将题干中的"locations"替换为"spots"和"places","like to use"替换为"favourite"和"usually"。这种同义词替换是雅思听力考试中常见的技巧,需要考生能够识别并理解这些同义表达。

questions.title: Researchers go to the locations the bee eaters like to use for   【   】 . They collect the pellets and take them to a   【   】 . for analysis. Here   【   】 is used to soften them, and the researchers look for the   【   】 of Asian bees in the pellets.
questions.analysis:
：

1. 题目要求填写进行分析的地点，而原文明确指出研究人员将收集到的小球带回实验室（laboratory）进行检查。

2. "Laboratory"与题目中的"analysis"（分析）直接相关，因为实验室是进行科学分析的标准场所。

3. 虽然原文提到研究人员在野外（field）收集样本，但实际的分析工作是在实验室进行的，这符合科学研究的标准流程。

4. "Lab"是"laboratory"的缩写形式，两者都被接受为正确答案，这体现了考试对同义词的灵活处理。

5. 答案句中的"examine the contents"（检查内容）与题目中的"analysis"（分析）在含义上是一致的，进一步确认了laboratory是正确答案。

因此，"laboratory"或其缩写"lab"是最符合题目要求和原文内容的答案。

questions.title: Researchers go to the locations the bee eaters like to use for   【   】 . They collect the pellets and take them to a   【   】 . for analysis. Here   【   】 is used to soften them, and the researchers look for the   【   】 of Asian bees in the pellets.
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写用来软化小球的物质。在原文中，研究人员明确提到他们添加水（adding water）来使小球湿润（moisten）并变软（make them softer）。水是唯一被提到的用于这个目的的物质。此外，题目要求填写一个单词，而"water"正好符合这个要求。它既是一个名词，又直接对应了题目中所描述的软化过程。其他可能出现的词如"moisten"是动词，不符合题目要求填写名词的条件。因此，"water"是最合适的答案。

questions.title: Researchers go to the locations the bee eaters like to use for   【   】 . They collect the pellets and take them to a   【   】 . for analysis. Here   【   】 is used to soften them, and the researchers look for the   【   】 of Asian bees in the pellets.
questions.analysis: 题目要求我们找出研究人员在食物球中寻找的亚洲蜜蜂的特定部分。原文中明确提到研究人员在寻找翅膀（wings）。这与整个研究过程相符：研究人员收集彩虹食蜂鸟吐出的食物球，软化后在显微镜下分析，特别寻找亚洲蜜蜂的翅膀。翅膀是识别蜜蜂种类的关键部分，因为昆虫的外骨骼被消化后，翅膀仍然保持完整。因此，"wings"（翅膀）是最合适的答案，直接对应研究人员在食物球中寻找的亚洲蜜蜂的特定部分。

questions.title: The benefit of this research is that the result is more   【   】 than searching for live Asian bees.
questions.analysis: 原文中直接使用了"reliable"这个词来描述研究结果的质量。文中明确表示，这种研究方法"much more reliable than trying to find live ones"（比试图寻找活体蜜蜂要可靠得多）。这正好对应了题干中要求填写的内容，即这项研究的好处是比搜寻活的亚洲蜜蜂更可靠（reliable）。此外，"reliable"完全符合题目要求的"ONE WORD ONLY"，而且准确表达了研究方法的优势。选择"reliable"不仅直接对应原文，还准确反映了科学研究中对方法可靠性的重视。

transcript.en: PROFESSOR: Good morning everyone. In today's seminar, Grant Freeman, a biologist who specialises in identifying insects, and who works for the Australian Quarantine Service, has come to talk to us about his current research work.
transcript.cn: 教授：大家早上好！在今天的研讨会中，我们邀请到了来自澳大利亚检疫局的生物学家、昆虫分类专家格兰特·弗里曼先生来谈一谈他最近的研究。

transcript.en: Right, well, over to you, Grant.
transcript.cn: 好的，那接下来就交给你了，格兰特。

transcript.en: GRANT: Good morning, everyone. I'm sure that you know that the quarantine service regulates all food brought into Australia.
transcript.cn: 格兰特：大家早上好！众所周知，检疫局的工作是检查被带入澳洲的食物。

transcript.en: Well, obviously they want to protect Australia from diseases that might come in with imported goods, but they also want to prevent insect pests from being introduced into the country, and that's where I have a part to play.
transcript.cn: 这样做的目的显然是想要保护澳洲民众免受进口食物带来的疾病之苦。但与此同时，我们还有另一项工作，那就是防止害虫入境。而这也正是我的主要工作。

transcript.en: Anyway, my current research involves trying to find a particular type of bee, the Asian Honey Bee, and finding out whether there are any of them around in various states of Australia.
transcript.cn: 总之，我目前的工作是寻找一种特殊的蜜蜂——亚洲蜜蜂。看看它们是否存在于澳洲各州。

transcript.en: We discovered a few of them in Queensland once and eradicated them.
transcript.cn: 我们在昆士兰发现了一些，并且将它们消灭了。

transcript.en: Now, we're pretty keen to make sure that there aren't any more getting in, particularly to New South Wales and other states.
transcript.cn: 现在，我们非常希望确认没有新的亚洲蜜蜂入境，尤其是出现在新南威尔士和其他州。

transcript.en: STUDENT 1: What's wrong with Asian Honey Bees?
transcript.cn: 学生1：亚洲蜜蜂怎么了？

transcript.en: Are they so different from Australian bees?
transcript.cn: 它们和澳洲蜜蜂区别很大吗？

transcript.en: GRANT: Well, in fact, they look almost the same, but they are infested with mites - microscopic creatures which live on them, and which can seriously damage our own home-grown bees, or could even wipe them out.
transcript.cn: 格兰特：这个嘛，它们看起来和澳洲蜜蜂差不多，但它们身上携带着很多螨虫，这些寄生虫会严重的伤害澳洲蜜蜂，甚至会导致它们的灭绝。

transcript.en: PROFESSOR: Well, what would happen if Australian bees died out?
transcript.cn: 教授：那如果澳洲蜜蜂都灭绝了将会有什么样的后果呢？

transcript.en: GRANT: Well, the honey from Australian bees is of excellent quality, much better than the stuff the Asian bees produce.
transcript.cn: 格兰特：嗯，澳洲蜜蜂产的蜂蜜质量很好，比亚洲蜜蜂产的蜜好很多。

transcript.en: In fact, Australia exports native Queen bees to a large number of countries because of this.
transcript.cn: 实际上因为这个原因，澳大利亚向很多国家都出口蜂后。

transcript.en: When the European Honey Bee was first discovered out in the bush, we found they made really unpleasant honey and they were also too big to pollinate many of our native flowers here in Australia.
transcript.cn: 当我们在灌木丛中首次发现欧洲蜜蜂的时候，我们发现它们产的蜂蜜质量很差，而且它们的体型太大了，无法为澳洲当地的很多花授粉。

transcript.en: STUDENT 2: That must have had a devastating effect on the natural flora.
transcript.cn: 学生2：那肯定对当地的植物产生毁灭性的影响。

transcript.en: Did you lose any species?
transcript.cn: 有没有因此而灭绝的物种呢？

transcript.en: GRANT: No, we managed to get them under control before that happened but if Asian bees got in there could be other consequences.
transcript.cn: 没有，在悲剧发生之前我们成功的控制了状况。但如果亚洲蜜蜂出现，那后果就不一样了。

transcript.en: We could lose a lot of money because you might not be aware, but it's estimated that native bees' pollination of flower and vegetable crops is worth 1.2 billion dollars a year.
transcript.cn: 我们很可能因为没有意识到它们的存在会导致巨大的经济损失。据估计每年通过本地蜜蜂授粉而生产的花卉和蔬菜的价值大约是12亿美元。

transcript.en: So in a way they're the farmers' friend.
transcript.cn: 因此，某种程度上它们是农民的朋友。

transcript.en: Oh, and another thing is, if you're stung by an Asian Honey Bee, it can produce an allergic reaction in some people; so they're much more dangerous than native bees.
transcript.cn: 对了，还有一点，如果被亚洲蜜蜂蜇伤了，一些人会出现过敏反应，所以它们比本地蜜蜂更加危险。

transcript.en: PROFESSOR: How will you know if Asian bees have entered Australia?
transcript.cn: 教授：你是通过什么方法来知道是否有亚洲蜜蜂进入澳大利亚呢？

transcript.en: GRANT: We're looking at the diet of the bird called the Rainbow Bee Eater.
transcript.cn: 格兰特：通过观察一种名为彩虹蜂虎的鸟类的饮食状况。

transcript.en: The Bee Eater doesn't care what it eats, as long as they're insects.
transcript.cn: 这种鸟吃各种昆虫，从不挑食。

transcript.en: But the interesting thing about this bird is that we are able to analyse exactly what it eats and that's really helpful if we're looking for introduced insects.
transcript.cn: 关于这种鸟有意思的地方在于，我们能够分析它们到底吃了些什么，而这能够帮助我们寻找入境的昆虫。

transcript.en: PROFESSOR: How come?
transcript.cn: 教授：这是怎么做到的呢？

transcript.en: GRANT: Because insects have their skeletons outside their bodies, so the Bee Eaters digest the meat from the inside.
transcript.cn: 格兰特：因为昆虫的体外有一层壳，蜂虎消化的是昆虫体内的肉质。

transcript.en: Then they bring up all the indigestible bits of skeleton and, of course, the wings in a pellet - a small ball of waste material which they cough up.
transcript.cn: 然后将所有不能消化的部分，包括翅膀弄成一个小球吐出来。

transcript.en: PROFESSOR: That sounds a bit unpleasant.
transcript.cn: 教授：这听上去有些恶心。

transcript.en: So, how do you go about it?
transcript.cn: 你们是怎么进行研究的呢？

transcript.en: GRANT: In the field we track down the Bee Eaters and find their favourite feeding spots, you know, the places where the birds usually feed.
transcript.cn: 格兰特：在野外我们追踪蜂虎并且寻找他们的捕食地点。

transcript.en: It's here that we can find the pellets.
transcript.cn: 我们在那些地方能够找到这些小球。

transcript.en: We collect them up and take them back to the laboratory to examine the contents.
transcript.cn: 我们收集这些小球，然后带回实验室分析里面的成分。

transcript.en: PROFESSOR: How do you do that?
transcript.cn: 教授：怎么分析呢？

transcript.en: GRANT: The pellets are really hard, especially if they have been out in the sun for a few days so, first of all, we treat them by adding water to moisten them and make them softer.
transcript.cn: 格兰特：这些小球很硬，尤其是在太阳下晒过几天以后。所以我们得先用水把它们泡软。

transcript.en: Then we pull them apart under the microscope.
transcript.cn: 然后我们将它们弄开放在显微镜下观察。

transcript.en: Everything's all scrunched up but we're looking for wings so we just pull them all out and straighten them.
transcript.cn: 所有的东西都揉成一团了，但是我们要找的只是翅膀。我们把它们弄出来然后展平。

transcript.en: Then we identify them to see if we can find any Asian bee wings.
transcript.cn: 然后我们通过翅膀来辨认亚洲蜜蜂的踪迹。

transcript.en: PROFESSOR: And how many have you found?
transcript.cn: 教授：你们到现在发现多少了？

transcript.en: GRANT: So far our research shows that Asian bees have not entered Australia in any number - it's a good result and much more reliable than trying to find live ones as evidence of introduced insects.
transcript.cn: 目前的研究表明澳大利亚境内还没有亚洲蜜蜂的踪迹。这个结果十分乐观，而且这种方法也比直接寻找活的蜜蜂更可靠。

transcript.en: PROFESSOR: Well, that's fascinating! Thank you, Grant, for those insights.
transcript.cn: 教授：那真是太棒了！谢谢你，格兰特，来跟我们分享这么多知识。

transcript.en: I hope that you might inspire some of our students here to conduct some similar experiments.
transcript.cn: 希望你的研究能激励我们这儿的学生也进行类似的实验。

groups.title: Research on questions about doctors
groups.desc:
Questions 31-36
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: In order to set up her research programme, Shona got

options.title: A
options.content: advice from personal friends in other countries.

options.title: B
options.content: help from students in other countries.

options.title: C
options.content: information from her tutor's contacts in other countries.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是B，因为原文明确提到研究者联系了海外部门的研究生（postgraduates）来帮助她组织研究。"Postgraduates"指的是研究生，属于学生类别，因此最符合选项B"来自其他国家的学生的帮助"。这个答案准确反映了作者如何设立她的研究计划，即通过联系其他国家的学生来协助她的研究。

questions.title: What types of people were included in the research?

options.title: A
options.content: young people in their first job

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B（men who were working）。研究者明确表示在当前阶段只关注在职男性，而将其他群体（如未就业人群和在职女性）的研究安排在之后。这表明研究的主要对象是在职男性，符合选项B的描述。

questions.title: Shona says that in her questionnaire her aim was

options.title: A
options.content: to get a wide range of data.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A "to get a wide range of data"。原文中明确表示作者在设计问卷时的目标是"include as much variety as possible"（包含尽可能多的多样性）。这与选项A "to get a wide range of data"（获得广泛的数据）的含义最为接近。原文中的"rather than limiting it to one specific point"（而不是将其限制在一个特定点上）进一步强调了作者想要获得广泛数据的意图。这种表述与选项A完全一致，表明作者的目的是获取广泛的、多样化的数据。

questions.title: What do Shona's initial results show about medical services in Britain?

options.title: A
options.content: Current concerns are misrepresented by the press.

options.title: B
options.content: Financial issues are critical to the government.

options.title: C
options.content: Reforms within hospitals have been unsuccessful.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A。原文中指出，尽管新闻报道说人们对医疗服务不满意，但实际上人们主要担心的是医院的三级医疗。这表明媒体对当前人们的担忧存在误报或夸大。选项A准确概括了这一点，指出"当前的担忧被媒体错误呈现"。这要求考生能够理解复杂的句子结构，识别出文中表达的对比关系（新闻报道 vs 实际情况），并正确理解"misrepresent"的含义。

questions.title: Shona needs to do further research in order to

options.title: A
options.content: present the government with her findings.

options.title: B
options.content: decide the level of extra funding needed.

options.title: C
options.content: identify the preferences of the public.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C选项，"identify the preferences of the public"（确定公众的偏好）。答案句中提到研究者需要进行更广泛的研究，以"establish if people want"（确定人们想要什么），这直接对应了选项C中的"identify the preferences"（确定偏好）。研究的目的是要了解公众对医疗服务的偏好，而不是向政府提交findings（发现）或决定资金水平。

questions.title: Shona has learnt from the research project that

options.title: A
options.content: it is important to plan projects carefully.

options.title: B
options.content: people do not like answering questions.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C "colleagues do not always agree"（同事并不总是意见一致）。从答案句中我们可以看出，Shona原本认为自己的研究设计很好，但后来发现并不是她部门里的每个人都持相同看法。这清楚地表明同事之间存在意见分歧。这个发现对Shona来说是意外的，也是她从这次研究中学到的重要一课。这个答案反映了研究过程中的一个重要认识：即使在同一个部门内，对同一件事的看法也可能存在差异。这种认识对于学术研究和团队合作都很重要，因为它强调了开放心态和有效沟通的必要性。

groups.desc:
Questions 37-40
Which statement applies to each of the following people who were interviewed by Shona?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to questions 37-40.

list.title: C
list.content: refused to tell Shona about their job

list.title: D
list.content: kept changing their mind about participating

questions.title: a person interviewed in the street
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B（decided to stop participating）。原文中提到一位在城里购物时被接近的受访者在第二轮采访时决定退出（"decided to pull out"）。这里的"out shopping in town"是题干中"interviewed in the street"的同义替换，而"decided to pull out"则直接对应了选项B中的"decided to stop participating"。这个例子被描述为"odd cases"，表明这是一个特殊情况，符合题目要求的特定受访者描述。

questions.title: an undergraduate at the university
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是F（was worried about confidentiality）。原文中提到一名一年级学生（即本科生）要求确保他们的名字不会被追踪到，这明确表明该学生对保密性感到担忧。"wanted reassurance that no names would be traceable"直接对应了选项F中的"worried about confidentiality"。这个答案考察了考生对表达担忧和保密概念的理解能力，以及识别"undergraduate"和"first-year student"同义关系的能力。

questions.title: a colleague in her department
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D（kept changing their mind about participating）。从原文中我们可以看到，说话者的同事反复改变主意：先说愿意参与，然后又不愿意，最后又同意了。这种行为正是"不断改变主意"的典型表现。这个例子完美地符合选项D的描述，展示了这位同事在参与调查时犹豫不决的态度。

questions.title: a tutor in a foreign university
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C（refused to tell Shona about their job）。在原文中，法国大学的教师在回答了许多问题后，拒绝透露他的具体工作内容。这直接对应了选项C中"refused to tell Shona about their job"的描述。这个例子清楚地展示了一个外国大学教师在被问及工作细节时拒绝回答的情况，完全符合题目要求和选项描述。

transcript.en: I've been doing some research into what people in Britain think of doctors, the ones who work in general practice - the first call for medical care - and comparing this with the situation in a couple of other countries.
transcript.cn: 我一直在研究英国人对那些从事一般诊疗，服务于医疗一线的医生的看法，并将结果与其他几个国家进行比较。

transcript.en: I want to talk about the rationale behind what I decided to do.
transcript.cn: 我想谈一谈我做这项研究背后的基本原理。

transcript.en: Now I had to set up my programme of research in three different countries so I approached postgraduates in my field in overseas departments, contacting them by email, to organize things for me at their end.
transcript.cn: 我的项目要在三个不同的国家展开研究，所以我去找了相关专业的海外研究生。通过邮件和他们沟通，让他们帮助我进行研究。

transcript.en: I thought I would have trouble recruiting help but in fact everyone was very willing and sometimes their tutors got involved too.
transcript.cn: 我原以为找人帮忙会不太容易，但没想到所有人都很有兴趣，甚至有时候他们的导师也参与了进来。

transcript.en: I had to give my helpers clear instructions about what kind of sample population I wanted them to use.
transcript.cn: 我必须明确地告诉他们我需要他们调查什么样的人口样本。

transcript.en: I decided that people under 18 should be excluded because most of them are students or looking for their first job, and also I decided at this stage just to focus on men who were in employment, and set up something for people who didn't have jobs and for employed women later on as a separate investigation.
transcript.cn: 我决定将18岁以下的人群排除在外，因为这些人大部分是学生或正在寻找第一份工作。另外我决定在这个阶段我只关注男性就业者，之后将没有工作的人和女性工作者作为后续另一个调查的重点。

transcript.en: I specifically wanted to do a questionnaire, and interviews with a focus group.
transcript.cn: 具体而言我想做一份问卷调查，然后对一个焦点小组进行访谈。

transcript.en: With the questionnaire, rather than limiting it to one specific point, I wanted to include as much variety as possible.
transcript.cn: 我尤其想要采用调查问卷，而不是只关注一个要点，这是因为我希望增加一些多样性。

transcript.en: I know questionnaires are a very controlled way to do things but I thought I could do taped interviews later on to counteract the effects of this.
transcript.cn: 我知道问卷是一种比较受限的方法，但我认为我能通过后面的录音访谈来抵消这种影响。

transcript.en: And the focus group may also prove useful in future, by targeting subjects I can easily return to, as the participants tend to be more involved.
transcript.cn: 在采访中加入一些今后可以轻松再次采访的对象，这样焦点小组访谈在将来也会继续发挥作用，因为他们更愿意参与到实验中。

transcript.en: So I'm just collating the results now.
transcript.cn: 现阶段我正在收集调研的结果。

transcript.en: At the moment it looks as if, in the UK, despite the fact that newspapers continually report that people are unhappy with medical care, in fact it is mainly the third level of care, which takes place in hospitals, that they are worried about.
transcript.cn: 根据目前的调查结果，尽管英国的报纸一直在报导民众对医疗的不满，但大家担忧的对象主要是在医院里进行的第三级医疗。

transcript.en: Government reforms have been proposed at all levels and although their success is not guaranteed, long-term hospital care is in fact probably less of an issue than the media would have us believe.
transcript.cn: 政府已经提出了各级医疗改革措施，虽然这些改革是否会获得成功还属未知，但长期医院医疗服务并不像媒体所宣传的那么令人堪忧。

transcript.en: However, I've still got quite a bit of data to look at.
transcript.cn: 可是还有一些数据我们需要关注一下。

transcript.en: Certainly I will need to do more far-reaching research than I had anticipated in order to establish if people want extra medical staff invested in the community, or if they want care to revert to fewer, but larger, key medical units.
transcript.cn: 显然我需要做比我预期更加深入的研究来了解人们是否需要在社区里增加医护人员，亦或是希望基础医疗单位更大一些但数量少一些。

transcript.en: The solution may well be something that can be easily implemented by those responsible in local government, with central government support of course.
transcript.cn: 在中央政府的支持下地方政府的官员就可以采纳并实施这些建议。

transcript.en: This first stage has proved very valuable though.
transcript.cn: 第一阶段的工作被证明是很有价值的。

transcript.en: I was surprised by how willing most of the subjects were to get involved in the project - I had expected some unwillingness to answer questions honestly.
transcript.cn: 大多数调查对象都特别愿意参与到这个项目中，这实在是让我感到意外。我原以为会有不少人不愿真实的回答问题。

transcript.en: But I was taken aback and rather concerned that something I thought I'd set up very well didn't necessarily seem that way to everyone in my own department.
transcript.cn: 但实际情况有点出乎我的意料，而且我现在还有些担心之前我觉得设计挺好的地方在我自己部门同事的眼中看起来并非如此。

transcript.en: I thought you might also be interested in some of the problems I encountered in collecting my data.
transcript.cn: 我觉得你们可能会对我在采集数据的过程中碰到的一些问题感兴趣。

transcript.en: There were odd cases that threw me - one of the subjects who I had approached while he was out shopping in town, decided to pull out when it came to the second round.
transcript.cn: 我遇到过不少奇怪的例子。有一个我的调查对象，我第一次采访他的时候他正在城里购物。结果到第二轮的时候他突然决定退出了。

transcript.en: It was a shame as it was someone who I would like to have interviewed more closely.
transcript.cn: 这实在太糟糕了，因为他本来是我打算做深访的对象之一。

transcript.en: And one of the first-year students I interviewed wanted reassurance that no names would be traceable from the answers.
transcript.cn: 还有一个大一的学生要求我们保证在答案中不会出现姓名信息。

transcript.en: I was so surprised, because they think nothing of telling you about themselves and their opinions in seminar groups!
transcript.cn: 这实在令我太吃惊了，因为他们加入了一个研讨会的团体，却不愿告诉你任何关于他们个人以及他们的观点的信息！

transcript.en: Then, one of the people that I work with got a bit funny.
transcript.cn: 然后还有一个人有点意思。

transcript.en: The questions were quite personal and one minute he said he'd do it, then the next day he wouldn't, and in the end he did do it.
transcript.cn: 调查的问题涉及到一些个人隐私；一开始他说他愿意回答，然后第二天又反悔了，结果到最后他还是参与了。

transcript.en: It's hard not to get angry in that situation but I tried to keep focused on the overall picture in order to stay calm.
transcript.cn: 在这种情况下想不生气很难，为了保持冷静我只能尽量把注意力集中在项目整体上面。

transcript.en: The most bizarre case was a telephone interview I did with a teacher at a university in France.
transcript.cn: 最奇葩的一个案例是我对法国一个大学老师进行的电话采访。

transcript.en: He answered all my questions in great detail- but then when I asked how much access he had to dangerous substances he wouldn't tell me exactly what his work involved.
transcript.cn: 他细致的回答了我的所有问题，但是当我问到他再多大程度上会接触到一些危险物质的时候他却不肯告诉我他到底是干什么工作的。

transcript.en: It's a real eye-opener ...
transcript.cn: 这真实太神奇了……
